payload.claim_text: 116th St. is 60' wide, curb to curb – Each direction has a travel lane and a combined travel/parking lane.
payload.description: Existing roadway configuration on 116th Street

payload.claim_text: Manhattan Av. is 42' wide, curb to curb – Each direction has one (1) combined travel/parking lane
payload.description: Existing roadway configuration on Manhattan Avenue

payload.claim_text: Pleasant Av. is 60' wide, curb to curb – Each direction has a combined travel/parking lane. The block b/n E. 119th & 120th Sts. has a standard 5' bike lane in both directions
payload.description: Existing roadway configuration on Pleasant Avenue

payload.claim_text: Vision Zero Priority Corridor, VZP Zone (between Malcolm X Blvd. and 3rd Av.), with three VZP Intersections: Malcolm X Blvd. & W. 116th St., Lexington Av. & E. 116th St., 1st Av. and E. 116th St.
payload.description: Vision Zero Priority designations in study area

payload.claim_text: Truck Route from Adam Clayton Powell Jr. Blvd. to 1st Av.
payload.description: Truck route designation along 116th Street
Community Boards 9, 10, & 11: Discuss existing conditions, needs & wants. Feedback will help shape DOT's draft proposal. Site visits & data collection.
Continue public outreach w/ CBs 9, 10 & 11, elected officials and other stakeholders. Conduct traffic analysis. Develop & share draft plans; feedback will help refine proposal. Share refined plan.
Potential Implementation in Summer/Fall 2025
Offset Bus Lane - dedicated red-painted bus lane offset from curb
Curbside Bus Lane - red-painted bus lane along the curb
Queue Jump Signal - signalized intersection with bus-only signal
Fewer Stops = Faster Buses - Improve Stop Spacing
Leaning Bars at bus stops
Benches at bus stops
Physical Accessibility - paved ramp leading to shelter
Bus Boarders - curb extension at bus stop
Pedestrian Islands
Painted & Concrete Curb Extensions
Mid-Block Crossings
Left Turn Bays

payload.claim_text: Other tools: Traffic signal timing, Transit Signal Priority (TSP), Bus lane camera enforcement – DOT stationary cameras – MTA on-bus cameras (ACE), Tree pits & streetscape improvements, Automated Pedestrian Signals (APS), DOT Art
payload.description: List of other tools considered for bus priority

payload.claim_text: Goals: Make bus service fast, reliable and on-time. Make corridor safer to walk & drive. Ensure access to businesses.
payload.description: Project goals for the 116th Street Bus Priority Review

payload.claim_text: Existing Conditions & Challenges: Traffic congestion, Double parking & vehicle access needs
payload.description: Existing challenges on the 116th Street corridor
